title_en: Introduction to The Book of the End: Great Trials and Tribulations
content_en:
The end of time occupies a central place in Islamic eschatology. The Quran references it in dozens of surahs; the Prophet ﷺ devoted extensive portions of his teaching to describing the signs, trials, and events that will precede and accompany the Day of Judgment. For Muslims, knowledge of the akhirah is not peripheral but essential — shaping how one understands this life, prioritizes actions, and maintains hope in Allah's ultimate justice.

This monumental work by Imam Ibn Kathir (701–774 AH / 1301–1373 CE) — the renowned scholar of Quran, hadith, and history — is one of the most comprehensive treatments of Islamic eschatology ever compiled. Drawing on the Quran, thousands of authenticated hadiths, and the accumulated scholarship of earlier generations, Ibn Kathir systematically addresses the full panorama of the end times: the lesser and greater signs of the Hour, the trials of the Dajjal, the descent of 'Isa (Jesus), the emergence of Ya'juj and Ma'juj, the Trumpet blast, the gathering and resurrection, the Bridge over Hellfire, the intercession of the Prophet ﷺ, the entry of the believers into Paradise, and the eternal torment of those in Hellfire.

Ibn Kathir applies his characteristic hadith methodology throughout — evaluating chains of narration, distinguishing authentic from weak or fabricated reports, and reconciling apparent contradictions in the texts. The result is not a work of speculation but a disciplined scholarly assembly of what Allah and His Messenger have authentically revealed about the unseen realities of the hereafter.

This is a book for serious reflection. The descriptions it contains are meant to move the reader: to sharpen their awareness of mortality, to illuminate the consequence of this life's choices, and to kindle genuine longing for the mercy of Allah. As Ibn Kathir himself reminds us, the Hour is coming — and it is closer than we think.
